title: Holloway Settlement
content:
The Holloway Settlement (2009) was a diplomatic agreement in which Greta Holloway, peace delegate of Republic of Dorsaly, helped finalize terms at Glimmerford.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 2010 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Glimmerford basin.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Holloway Settlement as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Greta Holloway's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Signing and terms.

The Holloway Settlement was signed in Glimmerford on 14 September 2009. The agreement established trade corridors and a joint commission chaired initially by Greta Holloway.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Glimmerford conference hall include Greta Holloway's opening address and the ratification roll call.
